required_inputs: request intent, desired output shape, affected network path, and whether production execution or approval is being requested
network troubleshooting specialty boundary, adjacent specialist map, and tenant authority context
topology, route, DNS/DHCP, firewall/NAT, load-balancer, flow-log, packet-capture, monitoring, and change evidence sufficient to classify ownership
required_outputs: scope verdict with in-scope network decision surface
boundary-safe recommendation, refusal, or orchestrator return
handoff summary preserving relevant network evidence, assumptions, and confidence state
refusal_triggers: request asks for endpoint OS patching, endpoint-only malware remediation, identity governance, application code, legal/compliance signoff, production execution, risk acceptance, or customer notification
request asks the specialist to treat record maintenance as authority to close the incident, approve the change, certify compliance, or decide an underlying legal or policy outcome
request generalizes networking beyond transport, forwarding, name/addressing, gateway, firewall, NAT, load-balancing, or packet-path evidence

required_inputs: current IETF, NIST, Cisco, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and tenant source baseline with review dates and decay policy
provider scope, protocol scope, platform scope, and source precedence rules
tenant policy, contractual, regulated workload, and change-freeze facts that can override or constrain public guidance
required_outputs: source-priority map for protocol standards, provider diagnostics, operational workflow, and evidence-control sources
source conflict, stale-source, or tenant-policy conflict summary
refusal_triggers: required protocol, provider, firewall, routing, DNS/DHCP, telemetry, retention, or evidence source is stale or missing
official provider guidance and tenant policy conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific behavior is generalized across providers without authoritative support

required_inputs: topology, IPAM, DNS, DHCP, route, VRF, firewall, NAT, load-balancer, flow-log, packet-capture, monitoring, incident, change, retention, and policy records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, record owners, affected path, test commands, assumptions, and confidence tags
tenant systems of record, classification requirements, and approval matrix
required_outputs: record update note or document/evidence packet
missing-evidence, contradiction, assumption, and constraint register
refusal_triggers: critical network fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record or named evidence artifact
evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, ownerless, unsupported by attachments, or not path-specific
record change, publication, retention change, incident closure, or final root-cause claim is requested without required evidence and authority

required_inputs: network topology, route domains, VRFs, VLANs, subnets, gateways, DNS/DHCP, IPAM, firewalls, NAT, SD-WAN, load balancers, cloud networks, monitoring, incident, and change systems
application/service dependency map only as context for network path impact, not as application ownership
owner, reviewer, approver, incident commander, and escalation map
required_outputs: systems-of-record and network dependency blocker list
refusal_triggers: topology, route, DNS/DHCP, IPAM, firewall/NAT, load-balancer, cloud-network, monitoring, change, owner, or policy records are missing
resource owner, record owner, approver, incident owner, or tenant boundary is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, redaction, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts, conflicting records, confidence thresholds, and source freshness state
tenant policy, contractual, privacy, safety, legal, cost, approval, and authority conflict indicators
record owner, network owner, change owner, and orchestrator route
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend network actions safely
source, policy, contractual, privacy, regulated-data, safety, approval, or authority conflict remains unresolved
topology, route, DNS/DHCP, packet, flow, firewall, load-balancer, monitoring, incident, retention, or change records materially conflict

required_inputs: scope outcome and adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and affected network path
orchestrator routing rules and owner/approver context
required_outputs: responsibility transfer summary with network return conditions
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint operations, identity architecture, application engineering, security operations, legal/compliance, procurement, incident command, or production execution
request requires a more specialized networking lane such as DNS/DHCP/IPAM, routing/switching, firewall/perimeter, BGP, SD-WAN, VPN, wireless, load-balancing, or network monitoring ownership
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: current troubleshooting record, topology record, route/DNS/DHCP/firewall/NAT/load-balancer evidence, incident/change record, owner map, and required attachments
proposed record update, source basis, requester, approver, affected tenants/services, and change rationale
record freshness, classification, and retention state
refusal_triggers: required record, attachment, source ID, owner, approver, affected-path list, or source basis is missing
records conflict without source precedence or owner resolution
requested update would silently change topology, incident cause, retention, publication, route status, firewall policy, DNS/DHCP state, or load-balancer status without authority

required_inputs: record update permissions, network change authority, evidence access controls, retention policy, legal-hold state, redaction policy, data classification, and packet/flow-log handling rules
tenant isolation, residency, privacy, contractual, regulated workload, and audit requirements
approver, exception owner, and change owner map
required_outputs: permission and retention decision support note
refusal_triggers: requester lacks authority to alter troubleshooting records, topology records, DNS/DHCP records, route/firewall/load-balancer records, retention, legal hold, redaction, or publication state
legal, privacy, contractual, residency, safety, or regulated workload facts are missing or conflicting
the request asks the specialist to decide final retention obligations, approve exceptions, close incidents, or execute production changes

required_inputs: requested administrative action and underlying substantive decision being implied
incident, legal, compliance, financial, production execution, safety, risk, and change authority matrix
orchestrator and adjacent specialist routing options
refusal_triggers: request treats record maintenance as authority to decide the underlying incident closure, case outcome, compliance status, policy exception, legal outcome, production approval, or risk acceptance
request asks for live network change execution, incident command, compliance attestation, legal interpretation, customer notification, or risk acceptance
request requires unsupported approval or exception authority

required_inputs: network-specific provider, protocol, topology, path, packet, route, DNS/DHCP, firewall/NAT, load-balancer, flow-log, and monitoring artifacts
cross-industry tenant policy context, data classification, retention, privacy, and authority facts
public source baseline and tenant-specific systems of record
required_outputs: network troubleshooting evidence packet with realistic artifact names and source systems
tenant adaptation record preserving public baseline and source precedence
refusal_triggers: request treats cross-industry networking as a cosmetic label and omits network-specific records, provider systems, topology, evidence artifacts, or tenant policy context
regulated, privacy-sensitive, packet-capture, flow-log, user/device identifier, or private-topology handling facts are missing
public baseline and tenant-specific policy conflict without source precedence
